proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2
; LINE: 83
; PROVEN: transitionCount >= 0.
; transitionCount starts at 0 and only increments (never decrements).
; Model: sum of two binary contributions. Negation is unsat.
(declare-const c1 Int)
(declare-const c2 Int)
(declare-const transition_count Int)
; Code transitions: per-assertion contributions are in {0, 1}
(assert (>= c1 0))
(assert (<= c1 1))
(assert (>= c2 0))
(assert (<= c2 1))
; Code transition: transitionCount accumulates contributions
(assert (= transition_count (+ c1 c2)))
; Negation: transition_count is negative
(assert (< transition_count 0))
(check-sat)
; expect: unsat — sum of non-negative terms cannot be negative

violations.claim: REACHABLE VIOLATION: the 2-variable syntactic threshold does not guarantee
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 83
; REACHABLE VIOLATION: the 2-variable syntactic threshold does not guarantee
; semantic non-vacuousness. An assertion (= x y) references 2 declared vars,
; meeting refs_in_assert >= 2, so transitionCount = 1 and isVacuous = false.
; Yet (assert (= x y)) is trivially sat (x=0, y=0) with no code model present.
(declare-const num_declares Int)
(declare-const refs_in_only_assert Int)
(declare-const transition_count Int)
; Code transition: block declares 2 variables (e.g., x and y)
(assert (= num_declares 2))
; Code transition: the single assertion references both declared variables
(assert (= refs_in_only_assert num_declares))
; Code transition: refs_in_only_assert >= 2 fires the if-body → transition_count = 1
(assert (= transition_count 1))
; isVacuous = (transition_count == 0) = false → block is NOT filtered
; Violation: transition_count = 1 yet the only assertion is a free-variable equality,
; trivially sat — no code transition is modeled
(assert (> transition_count 0))
(check-sat)
; sat — (= x y) passes the syntactic filter while remaining semantically vacuous
